agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory, non-negotiable requirements about whether courier GPS data should be exposed to customers.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Last-Mile Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver fulfilling last-mile delivery to the customer's address"},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"GPS coordinates of the courier's device, updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery"},{"term":"Delivery window","definition":"The time period during which a courier is actively in transit to a customer's delivery address"},{"term":"Location sharing","definition":"The act of transmitting courier GPS data to customer-facing interfaces"},{"term":"Driver retention","definition":"The ability to keep couriers on the platform; a critical supply-side metric in logistics"}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Courier location data is only relevant during an active delivery — no location data may be shared before pickup or after delivery completion.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Sharing location outside an active delivery violates courier privacy and has no business justification. This is a data-integrity and privacy constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery with status other than 'in_transit', when the customer requests courier location, then the system returns no location data."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Courier location data must be collected from driver devices during active deliveries for internal operational use (routing, ETA calculation, dispatch optimization).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without location data the platform cannot perform core logistics operations like ETA estimation and route optimization. This is a system-integrity requirement independent of the customer-visibility question.","testable_assertion":"Given a courier in 'in_transit' status, when the system polls for location, then a GPS fix is recorded in the operational data store."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The degree of location detail exposed to customers (exact coordinates vs. approximate zone vs. ETA-only) is a business policy decision that requires explicit resolution between Customer Success and Operations.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is the core policy conflict. A VP or cross-functional decision-maker can set the exposure level — it is not a physical or data-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a customer viewing delivery status, when location data is rendered, then the granularity matches the configured exposure policy (exact, approximate, or ETA-only)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Courier consent or acknowledgment of location-sharing policy must be obtained as part of onboarding or policy updates.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Labor law and privacy regulations in most jurisdictions require workers to be informed about location tracking and how their data is shared. Failure to obtain consent creates legal liability.","testable_assertion":"Given a courier who has not acknowledged the current location-sharing policy, when the system attempts to share their location with a customer, then sharing is blocked until acknowledgment is recorded."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A delivery is in 'in_transit' status","when":"The customer opens the tracking page","then":"The system displays delivery progress information at the granularity defined by the resolved location-sharing policy (pending stakeholder resolution)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A delivery has not yet been picked up or has already been completed","when":"The customer opens the tracking page","then":"No courier location data of any kind is displayed"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"The platform collects courier GPS data during an active delivery","when":"The data is stored","then":"It is available for internal operational use (ETA, routing) regardless of the customer-facing visibility policy"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A courier has not acknowledged the current location-sharing policy","when":"Their delivery is viewed by a customer","then":"The system falls back to ETA-only display with no location or map data"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier device loses GPS signal during delivery","expected_behavior":"System falls back to last-known position with a staleness indicator, or degrades to ETA-only. Never display stale coordinates as if they are current."},{"scenario":"Customer screenshots or records courier location data","expected_behavior":"Even approximate location data can be used to identify patterns. Policy must account for data minimization — showing less is always safer from a privacy standpoint."},{"scenario":"Courier is on a multi-stop route and visible to multiple customers simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Each customer should only see progress relevant to their own delivery, never the courier's full route or other customers' addresses."},{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for an extended period (e.g., traffic, break)","expected_behavior":"System should avoid exposing precise stationary location which could feel invasive. Progress status or ETA update is preferable."},{"scenario":"Regulatory jurisdiction requires or prohibits location sharing","expected_behavior":"Location-sharing policy must be configurable per jurisdiction to comply with local labor and privacy laws (e.g., GDPR, local labor surveillance regulations)."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"If couriers leave the platform due to surveillance concerns, driver acquisition costs increase significantly (typically 3-5x the cost of retention). Conversely, if customers cannot track deliveries, customer satisfaction and retention decline, increasing customer acquisition costs. Both sides carry real revenue risk.","operational":"Operations needs location data internally regardless of customer visibility. The question is purely about the customer-facing layer. A poorly chosen policy creates either driver churn (Operations concern) or customer churn (Customer Success concern). Both degrade delivery capacity and service quality.","user":"Customers expect delivery visibility — industry standard is at minimum ETA and progress indicators. Couriers expect reasonable privacy protections. Both user groups have legitimate needs that must be balanced.","risk":"HIGH. Deploying either team's requirement as-stated creates material business harm. Showing exact GPS coordinates risks driver attrition and potential labor/privacy legal exposure. Showing nothing risks competitive disadvantage and customer dissatisfaction. The unresolved conflict is itself a risk — building either version without resolution will waste engineering effort if the policy is reversed.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Directly affected — their delivery tracking experience depends on the resolution. Exact GPS gives maximum transparency; ETA-only gives minimum."},{"group":"Couriers / Drivers","impact":"Directly affected — their sense of privacy and workplace dignity is at stake. Driver retention is a critical supply-side constraint in logistics."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Owns customer satisfaction metrics. Needs a tracking feature that demonstrably improves NPS and reduces 'where is my order' support tickets."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Owns driver supply and retention metrics. Needs a policy that does not accelerate driver churn."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted but critically affected. Location data sharing has regulatory implications (GDPR, labor surveillance laws) that neither team has addressed."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Cannot begin implementation until the exposure policy is resolved. Building for exact GPS vs. approximate zone vs. ETA-only requires different architectural decisions."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct, unresolved contradiction between two stakeholder teams, both claiming non-negotiable positions. No decision-maker has adjudicated. Additionally, Legal/Compliance has not been consulted on what is even permissible. Confidence in defining the correct customer-facing behavior is very low until the conflict is resolved by someone with authority over both teams.","high_confidence_areas":["Location data must be collected for internal operational use regardless of customer visibility","No location sharing outside active delivery window","Courier consent is required for any location-sharing policy","The system should support configurable granularity so the policy can be changed without re-architecture"],"low_confidence_areas":["Regulatory and labor-law constraints on courier location sharing have not been assessed by Legal","Whether approximate location (e.g., street-level, zone-level) would satisfy Customer Success while mitigating Operations concerns is unknown","Industry benchmarks for what level of tracking drives customer satisfaction vs. what level triggers driver attrition have not been provided"],"assumptions":["Both teams are acting in good faith and represent real business concerns","There exists an executive or cross-functional authority who can resolve this conflict","The platform already collects courier GPS data for operational purposes"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires showing exact courier GPS coordinates to customers as a non-negotiable differentiator. Operations requires never showing courier location to customers as a non-negotiable driver-retention measure. These two requirements are mutually exclusive as stated — the system cannot simultaneously always show and never show courier location.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Operations","Couriers","Customers","Engineering"],"options":["A) Side with Customer Success: show exact GPS. Risk: accelerated driver attrition, potential legal exposure.","B) Side with Operations: show no location. Risk: competitive disadvantage, higher support volume.","C) Compromise: show approximate location (zone or street-level) or progress-bar with ETA, not exact coordinates. Requires both teams to accept a middle ground.","D) Tiered approach: let couriers opt-in to location sharing with incentives (e.g., higher visibility = priority dispatch). Requires both teams to accept partial implementation.","E) Defer customer-facing location entirely. Ship ETA-only tracking first, gather data on customer satisfaction and driver sentiment, then make a data-driven decision on location granularity."],"recommendation":"Option E first, then Option C. Ship ETA-only tracking immediately (no location data shared, Operations is satisfied, customers get meaningful tracking). Simultaneously run a pilot with approximate-zone visibility and measure both customer NPS impact and driver sentiment. This avoids committing to either extreme before data is available. A cross-functional decision-maker (VP/CPO) must own the final policy decision."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"Neither team has consulted Legal/Compliance on the permissibility of sharing courier location data with customers. In many jurisdictions, sharing employee/contractor real-time location with third parties has regulatory constraints. The entire debate may be moot if regulations prohibit exact GPS sharing regardless of business preference.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers","Customers","Customer Success","Operations"],"options":["A) Proceed without legal review and accept regulatory risk.","B) Block feature until Legal/Compliance provides guidance on permissible location-sharing granularity per jurisdiction."],"recommendation":"Option B. Legal review must happen before any customer-facing location feature ships. This is not optional — it is a compliance gate."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot implement 'show exact GPS at all times' as specified by Customer Success","business_rule_broken":"Directly contradicts Operations requirement and creates driver retention risk. No authority has resolved the conflict."},{"violation":"Cannot implement 'never show location' as specified by Operations","business_rule_broken":"Directly contradicts Customer Success requirement and competitive positioning. No authority has resolved the conflict."},{"violation":"No Legal/Compliance review has been conducted","business_rule_broken":"BR-4 (courier consent and regulatory compliance) cannot be satisfied without Legal input on what granularity of location sharing is permissible."}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory, non-negotiable requirements about whether courier GPS data should be exposed to customers.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Last-Mile Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver fulfilling last-mile delivery to the customer's address"},
